learningObjectives.title: Understand enterprise structure
learningObjectives.description: Explain how SAP models real-world business hierarchy.

learningObjectives.title: Identify key organizational units
learningObjectives.description: Differentiate client, company, company code, plant and storage location.

learningObjectives.title: Relate structure to SAP TM
learningObjectives.description: Describe how organizational units are used in TM planning and settlement.

sections.content: SAP Organizational Structure (Enterprise Structure) represents how a company is modeled inside the SAP system. It defines legal entities, business units, plants, storage locations and sales organizations, enabling SAP to process business transactions according to the organization's real-world hierarchy.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Enterprise Structure defines 'where' business transactions occur, while Organizational Management defines 'who' performs them.

sections.title: Enterprise Structure Components
rows: Highest organizational level representing an SAP system

sections.content: SAP Transportation Management integrates with organizational units from SAP S/4HANA. Plants, shipping points, warehouses and company codes are used during transportation planning, execution and freight settlement.

ABC Manufacturing has headquarters in Hyderabad with production plants in Chennai and Pune. Customer orders are received by the Sales Organization, fulfilled from Plants, shipped through Shipping Points and financially reported under the Company Code. SAP Enterprise Structure models this entire business hierarchy.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational retail company configured multiple Company Codes for different countries while sharing one Client. SAP TM planned transportation across multiple Plants and Shipping Points while freight settlement was posted to the correct Company Code.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Interviewers frequently ask the difference between Client, Company, Company Code and Plant. Always explain the hierarchy from highest level to lowest level with a practical business example.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Plant with Storage Location. A Plant represents a manufacturing or distribution facility, whereas a Storage Location represents a physical inventory area within a Plant.

items: Client is the highest organizational level.
Company Code is the legal accounting entity.
Plant represents a manufacturing or distribution facility.
Storage Location stores inventory inside a Plant.
SAP TM uses Enterprise Structure for planning, execution and settlement.
